parsed.summary: The cumulative v0.4.0→v0.4.6 diff introduces a new retry/backoff system (retry.mjs, 203 lines), a BigModel error dispatcher (bigmodel-errors.mjs, 168 lines), and significant modifications to glm-client.mjs, while simultaneously deleting process.mjs (67 lines removed). The diff stat shows no surviving test for process.mjs consumers or for integration between retry and glm-client under concurrent/partial-failure conditions. Without being able to inspect the actual code contents, three structural risks are flagged that warrant manual verification before a v0.4.7 ship decision.

findings.title: process.mjs deleted — potential broken imports at runtime
findings.body: The diff shows `scripts/lib/process.mjs` with 67 lines entirely removed and no replacement file listed. If any surviving module (glm-companion.mjs, glm-client.mjs, render.mjs, etc.) still contains a dynamic or static `import` referencing `./process.mjs` or `./lib/process.mjs`, the import will throw at runtime. This class of failure is silent in static analysis if imports are conditional or string-concatenated. The commit message for the deletion commit ('refactor: Bundle E+ simplify pass') suggests functions were inlined or moved, but without verifying every consumer this is a latent crash risk.
findings.recommendation: Grep the entire codebase for any remaining reference to 'process.mjs' (including dynamic imports, require, or string references). If any exist, either restore the needed exports or update the consumers. Add a CI check that validates all import targets resolve.

findings.title: New retry.mjs (203 lines) — idempotency and retry-storm risk unverified
findings.body: A brand-new retry/backoff module with 203 lines is the largest single addition. Retry logic that wraps API calls (especially to BigModel via glm-client.mjs) must guarantee that retried operations are idempotent. If a BigModel job-submission call succeeds server-side but the response is lost (network timeout), a retry could create duplicate jobs. The commit message 'feat: v0.4.6 BigModel error dispatch + auto retry/backoff' suggests automatic retry on errors, but automatic retry on non-idempotent endpoints without deduplication is a classic source of duplicate side-effects. Additionally, if retry counts or backoff caps are misconfigured, retry storms could exhaust API rate limits or budget.
findings.recommendation: 1) Verify that every call site wrapped by retry.mjs is idempotent or includes a deduplication key. 2) Ensure retry counts have hard upper bounds and that backoff is exponential with jitter. 3) Add integration tests simulating partial-response scenarios (timeout after server-side commit) to confirm no duplicate side-effects. 4) Log every retry attempt with enough context for post-incident analysis.

findings.title: bigmodel-errors.mjs error dispatch may silently swallow unrecognized errors
findings.body: A 168-line error classification module for BigModel API errors is new. Error dispatchers that pattern-match on known error codes or messages risk falling through to a default case that either logs and returns a generic error (swallowing diagnostic context) or, worse, returns normally. If the dispatcher catches an error and reclassifies it as non-retryable when the underlying cause is transient (or vice versa), the retry layer will make the wrong decision. The tests file (tests/bigmodel-errors.test.mjs, 189 lines) exists but only covers known error patterns — the unknown/unrecognized error path is the highest-risk blind spot.
findings.recommendation: Audit the default/fallthrough branch in the error dispatcher to ensure: 1) unrecognized errors are never silently downgraded to non-errors, 2) full error context (stack, headers, status code) is preserved in any reclassified error, 3) a test explicitly exercises an unrecognized/unexpected error shape to confirm safe propagation.

next_steps: Run `grep -r 'process\.mjs' scripts/` to confirm no dangling imports remain after the deletion.
Inspect retry.mjs call sites in glm-client.mjs to confirm each retried HTTP verb is idempotent or guarded by an idempotency key.
Read the default/fallthrough branch in bigmodel-errors.mjs and verify unrecognized errors propagate with full context rather than being swallowed.
Run the full test suite (`tests/*.test.mjs`) and confirm all pass before proceeding with v0.4.7 tagging.
